key_insight: Discrete-time IGO on multivariate Gaussians is shown to contract covariance to zero on strongly convex quadratic objectives, with mean convergence to the optimum under a bounded-condition-number condition.
engineering_relevance: This strengthens the theoretical foundation for covariance-adaptive black-box optimizers, helping connect IGO/CMA-ES-style practice with provable convergence in continuous spaces.
method: Analyze discrete-time IGO as natural-gradient updates in expectation-parameter coordinates of an exponential family
Specialize the theory to full-covariance multivariate Gaussian search distributions
Prove covariance collapse to zero and mean convergence under strong convexity, fixed learning rate, and quantile-based weights
evidence_signal: The paper presents a mathematical convergence analysis on strongly convex quadratic test functions rather than a numerical benchmark study.
watch_item: Mean convergence is guaranteed only when the condition number of a scaled covariance matrix stays bounded at sufficiently frequent iterations, so the result depends on a nontrivial regularity condition.
paper_title: Beyond IGO-Flow: Toward Convergence Analysis of IGO in Continuous Spaces
